game_meta.name: Counter-Strike 2
game_meta.tagline: 5v5 tactical FPS — buy economy, bomb objective, no respawn.

sides.attack: T (Terrorists) — plant the bomb on A or B site
sides.defense: CT (Counter-Terrorists) — prevent the plant or defuse

game_meta.character_vocab: agents (but mechanically all agents play identically; pick is cosmetic, not tactical)
game_meta.round_economy: Pistol (R1) → Anti-eco / Force buy / Eco / Half-buy / Full buy. Always check teammate money before deciding your buy.
game_meta.round_format: MR12 — first to 13 rounds wins. Halftime side swap after 12 rounds.
key_mechanics: Spray patterns: each rifle has a unique spray pattern that must be counter-pulled to control
One-tap headshots with rifles (AK / M4) at any range
Smokes are volumetric and persistent (~18 seconds); HE grenades can clear them
Bomb plant: 3.2s plant animation, 40s timer (35s with defuse kit + 5s = 10s defuse)
Movement: counter-strafe (release WASD before peeking) for instant accuracy
Sound cues: footsteps, weapon switches, reloads, defuse beeps are critical

mirage.name: Mirage

sites.name: A Site (Default / Triple)
common_t_executes: Default setup: smoke CT, smoke Stairs, smoke Jungle, flash over Palace, 3-man Ramp + 2-man Palace split
Fast A: Ramp rush with mid smoke for Connector, no utility wasted on Palace
Late A through Palace: split timing on Connector smoke + Palace flash, post-plant near Sandwich
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 CT + 1 Connector + 1 mid (window) + 2 B (apps + site)
Stack A: 3 A (1 CT, 1 Triple, 1 Connector) + 1 mid + 1 B if reading aggressive default
Aggressive mid: 1 window peek + Connector for early info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb in Default: hold from Sandwich, Tetris, and Ninja with smoke at Stairs.

sites.name: B Site (Apartments)
common_t_executes: Default Apps: smoke Window + Short, flash through Apps door, 4-man execute
Fast Apps: rush short + apps with one flash, no smokes — works on eco CTs
B through Mid: smoke Connector + Top-mid, split Apps + Short
common_ct_setups: Default: 2 B (1 Bench, 1 Kitchen) + 1 Window + 1 Connector + 1 A
Aggressive Apps: CT pushes Apps for early info, risky vs full buy
Stack B: 3 B + 1 mid + 1 A — if reading B-heavy
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb behind boxes: hold from Bench, Kitchen, Short with smoke covering CT retake angle.

mirage.mid_control: Mid is contested first 15s of every round. Window control (CT side) gives Connector + Top-mid. Window-take with smoke + flash is the standard T mid play.

inferno.name: Inferno

sites.name: A Site (Pit / Default)
common_t_executes: Default A: smoke CT, smoke Library, molly Arch, flash over Pit, 4-man Apartments + 1 Short
Apps push: smoke Library + Arch, flash + execute from Apps with no Short presence
Short A: smoke Arch + Pit, push Short for the Arch trade — needs Apartments control first
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Pit + 1 Arch + 1 Library + 1 Banana + 1 mid
Stack A: 3 A + 1 Banana + 1 mid
Aggressive Apps: CT pushes Apartments early for info — high risk
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb in Default: hold Apps, Short, Arch with molly over CT spawn rotation.

sites.name: B Site (Banana)
common_t_executes: Banana control: molly Car + Coffins, smoke CT + Dark, push 5-man through Banana
Default B: 4-man Banana + 1 mid info, control top-Banana, execute on CT smoke
Fake A real B: utility commit to Apps with 1 player, real 4-man B execute
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Car + 1 site + 1 mid + 2 A
Aggressive Banana: 2 CT push Banana for early info + early kill
Stack B: 3 B (Car, Sandbags, CT) + 1 mid + 1 A
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb behind Sandbags: hold from Banana flash-out angle, Dark, and CT spawn rotate.

key_smokes: CT smoke from Top of Banana
inferno.mid_control: Inferno mid is short/quiet. Mid serves as info-gathering for both sites. CT mid takes are rare.

anubis.name: Anubis

sites.name: A Site (Heaven / Connector)
common_t_executes: Palace + Connector split: flash Heaven, smoke CT, push from both sides
Default A: smoke CT, molly Connector, push Palace with 4
Fake mid real A: utility through mid, real execute Palace + Connector
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Heaven + 1 Connector + 1 Palace + 1 mid + 1 B
Stack A: 3 A + 1 mid + 1 B
Mid control: 2 CT contest mid early for info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb on Boxes: hold from Pillar, Big Window, and Heaven angle with smoke covering Connector.

sites.name: B Site (Bridge / Water)
common_t_executes: Bridge default: molly Default, smoke Heaven, push Bridge with 4 + 1 mid
Water flank: smoke B Doors, push Water for the flank trade
Default B execute: smoke Heaven + B Doors, push Walkway with mid info
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 B Default + 1 Heaven + 1 mid + 2 A
Aggressive Bridge: 2 CT push Bridge early for info
Stack B: 3 B + 1 mid + 1 A
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Default: hold from Tetris, Heaven, and Walkway with smoke covering CT push lane.

anubis.mid_control: Anubis mid is wide-open with multiple entries. Mid Doors take is the key opening play — control mid for both sites' rotation.

nuke.name: Nuke

sites.name: A Site (Upper / Heaven)
common_t_executes: Outside take + Squeaky: smoke Heaven, push Squeaky for upper site execute
Heaven take: lurk through Squeaky, flash Heaven, hold Heaven for the post-plant
Fast A: rush Outside + Squeaky with two flashes, no smokes — works on eco CTs
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Heaven + 1 Site + 1 Hut + 1 Outside + 1 B (Vents/Ramp)
Stack Outside: 2 CT contest Outside early for info
Aggressive Heaven: CT plays Heaven angle for the off-angle pick
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Site: hold Heaven + Hut + Ramp with smoke covering CT rotate from B.

sites.name: B Site (Lower / Vent)
common_t_executes: Vent execute: drop through Vent with smokes on Window + Lockers, push from above
Outside + Vent split: 3 Outside + 2 Vent for split timing execute
Tunnel rush: rush Tunnel with flashes, fast plant — risky vs full buy CT
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Vent + 1 Window + 1 mid + 2 A
Stack B: 3 B (Vent, Window, Site) + 1 mid + 1 A
Aggressive Outside: 2 CT push Outside early for info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb behind Box: hold Window + Lockers + Tunnel with smoke covering Vent.

nuke.mid_control: Nuke has no traditional mid — the 'mid' is Outside Yard, which connects A and B vertically. Outside control = both sites.

ancient.name: Ancient

sites.name: A Site (Heaven / Donut)
common_t_executes: Donut take: smoke Heaven, flash Cave, push Donut for site execute
Default A: smoke Heaven + CT, push Ramp with 4 + 1 mid
Temple split: 3 Temple + 2 Donut for split timing execute
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Heaven + 1 Donut + 1 mid + 1 Cave + 1 B
Stack A: 3 A + 1 mid + 1 B
Aggressive Cave: CT plays Cave early for the flank pick
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Default: hold Donut + Cave + Heaven with smoke covering CT rotate.

sites.name: B Site (Pillar)
common_t_executes: Default B: smoke Heaven, smoke Side, push Tunnel + House with 4
Fast B: rush Tunnel with one flash, no smokes — works on eco CTs
Mid split: 3 mid + 2 Tunnel for B split timing
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Heaven + 1 Pillar + 1 mid + 2 A
Stack B: 3 B + 1 mid + 1 A
Aggressive mid: 2 CT contest mid for info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Default: hold Stairs + Heaven + Side with smoke covering Tunnel retake.

ancient.mid_control: Ancient mid is the standard map's tightest mid. Mid control gives both A (via Temple) and B (via Tunnel).

vertigo.name: Vertigo

sites.name: A Site (Ramp / Heaven)
common_t_executes: Ramp default: smoke Heaven, smoke Connector, push Ramp with 4
B-site through A: split timing with B push + A push for late rotate
Fast Ramp: rush Ramp with two flashes — works on eco CTs
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Heaven + 1 Site + 1 Connector + 2 B
Stack A: 3 A + 1 mid + 1 B
Aggressive Connector: CT plays Connector for early info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Site: hold Heaven + Connector + Ramp with smoke covering CT spawn.

sites.name: B Site (Generator / Catwalk)
common_t_executes: Ladder Room rush: smoke Window + Catwalk, push from Ladder Room with 4
Default B: smoke CT + Catwalk, push T-Stairs with 4 + 1 mid
Fake A real B: 1 player Apps utility, 4-man real B execute
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Catwalk + 1 Site + 1 mid + 2 A
Stack B: 3 B + 1 mid + 1 A
Aggressive T-Stairs: CT pushes T-Stairs early for info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Site: hold Catwalk + Window + CT with smoke covering rotation.

vertigo.mid_control: Vertigo has tight mid. Mid control gives info on rotates. Ladder Room flank is the unique map play.

dust2.name: Dust 2

sites.name: A Site (Long / Short)
common_t_executes: Long default: smoke CT + Ramp, flash Long, push Long with 4 — risky if CT pushes Long
Short default: smoke Ramp + Site, push Short with 4 + 1 Long info
Long + Short split: 3 Long + 2 Short for split timing execute
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Long + 1 Short + 1 mid + 2 B (Tuns + Door)
Stack A: 3 A + 1 mid + 1 B
Aggressive Long: 2 CT push Long for early Long control
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Default: hold Long + Short + Ninja with smoke covering CT rotate.

sites.name: B Site (Tunnels / Door)
common_t_executes: Tunnels default: smoke Door + Window, push Tunnels with 4
Fast B: rush Tunnels with flashes — works on eco CTs
Window + Tunnels split: 3 Tunnels + 2 Door for split timing
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Door + 1 Site + 1 mid + 2 A
Stack B: 3 B + 1 mid + 1 A
Aggressive Tunnels: CT pushes Tunnels early for info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Back: hold from Tunnels exit, Window, and Door with smoke covering CT rotate.

dust2.mid_control: Dust 2 mid is the iconic Xbox + Catwalk fight. Mid control gives B Door + A Short rotations.

train.name: Train

sites.name: A Site (Z / Halls)
common_t_executes: Z + Ivy split: smoke Hell + Heaven, push Z + Ivy split timing
Halls default: smoke Halls + Z, push Halls with 4
T-Roof play: aggressive T-Roof for the early pick on Heaven
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Halls + 1 Ivy + 1 mid + 1 B + 1 floating
Stack A: 3 A + 1 mid + 1 B
Aggressive Ivy: CT pushes Ivy early for info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Site: hold Ivy + Halls + Heaven with smoke covering CT rotate.

sites.name: B Site (Upper / Lower)
common_t_executes: Lower default: smoke Pop Dog + Ladder, push Lower with 4
Upper take: rush Upper for the flank trade
Pop Dog split: 3 Lower + 2 Pop Dog for split timing
common_ct_setups: Default: 1 Pop Dog + 1 Site + 1 mid + 2 A
Stack B: 3 B + 1 mid + 1 A
Aggressive mid: 2 CT contest mid for early info
sites.post_plant_positions: Bomb at Back Train: hold from Ladder, Sandwich, and Pop Dog with smoke covering CT spawn.

train.mid_control: Train mid is layered. Pop Dog gives info on both sites' rotations.

agents.note: CS2 agents are cosmetic only — kit and gameplay are identical for all. The 'role' is the in-game position the player takes, not the agent skin selected.

Entry Fragger.description: First player into the site on execute. Trades aggression for utility coverage.
Entry Fragger.kit: AK-47 / M4A4 (rifle); flashbang for entry; smoke from teammate
common_mistakes: Pushing without a flash from teammate
Trying to clear all angles before teammates enter
Entry Fragger.ideal_use: Push first on team's flash, take the duel, die or trade. Survival is bonus.

Awper.description: Player on the AWP — controls long sightlines, holds map control angles.
Awper.kit: AWP (sniper) + Deagle / pistol; smoke for repositioning
common_mistakes: Holding an angle for too long after enemy knows position
Saving AWP on lost rounds (drop = round-losing)
Awper.ideal_use: Hold a long sightline early round (Mirage Window, Inferno Banana), trade for info, reposition after first kill.

Support / Lurker.description: Plays utility behind entry. Lurks for late-round flanks or trade kills.
common_mistakes: Throwing all utility before team executes (no late support)
Lurking too deep — can't trade entry
Support / Lurker.ideal_use: Pop smoke during execute, flash for trade, lurk apartments / connector for late-round trade frags.

Anchor.description: CT site holder. Plays the bomb site, doesn't rotate unless called.
common_mistakes: Rotating early — losing site to fake
Anchor.ideal_use: Hold a site angle, save molly for plant deny, trade with secondary CT on entry.

IGL.description: In-Game Leader. Calls mid-round adjustments and reads enemy economy.
common_mistakes: Calling the same default every round (predictable)
IGL.ideal_use: Read enemy buy from sound + kill feed, call mid-round adjustments, save utility for late-round trades.

silver: Spraying with mouse 1 held — no recoil control
Buying AWP on eco / force buy without team eco buy
Pushing common angles without flash / smoke
Crosshair placement too low or random (default)
Walking around the map (sprinting all the time, no audio control)
Buying armor only every other round (always buy full kevlar + helmet)
Saving rifle on lost rounds instead of dropping for teammate
No comms — full game in silence
Wasting nades — throwing every smoke without a plan
Crossfire-blind: peeking without checking teammate trade position
nova: Spray patterns not learned — pulling random direction
No crosshair placement discipline — looking at the ground while moving
Pre-aim at head level but at wrong distance (peeking at body level)
No utility coordination — random flashes / smokes during execute
Rotating too late — caught in mid on rotation
Eco round commits: trying to win pistol-tier round with AK eco
Holding angles past first kill — enemy knows position
No round economy: full buy after losing pistol = pre-loss
Saving without telling team — split economy
Crouching while shooting (loses accuracy on rifles)
mg: Crosshair placement on map geometry, not enemy spawns
Half-flash exposure — flashed teammate trade falls apart
Smoke timing on execute — late smoke = caught in spam
No retake utility on CT side (no molly for retake)
Aggressive AWP holds without escape plan
Defaults too aggressive — over-committed to one site
Trade window missed (3+ second gap between entry and follow-up)
dmg: Util usage on execute — out of order (smoke before flash + molly = wasted)
AWP picks on standard angles (not off-angles)
Half-buy spending — buying SMG without team plan
No anti-eco response (eco round CT side = passive default, T side = stack)
Bait CT on retake — peeking after teammate dies = trade fail
Round 1 pistol commit on every map (no economy thinking)
Crosshair placement on common angles — not adjusted for map
Lurker timing off — too early (no info), too late (trade missed)
No comm on save round protection — solo save = info denial fail
le_lem: Off-angle holds are obvious — enemy team has read them
Util execution timing — smoke fade timing wrong
No mid-round adjustment — sticking to fail default
Eco round commits — risk vs reward miscalculation
AWP rotates blocked by smoke timing
Plant position too aggressive — caught in retake spray
Late round info gathering — not adjusting from teammate death
Buy decision based on round count, not economy difference
Trade kills missed by 1 second timing window
Util coordination across team — overlapping utility waste
supreme_global: Pro-tier reads on enemy default — but predictable on own default
Util discipline gaps — 1 wasted smoke per round
Mid-round comm overload — too many calls, lost info
Off-angle pre-aim — not adjusting per round
Lurker map info not relayed in time
Round economy: 1 dollar shy on full buy = 4-utility instead of 5
AWP positioning on retakes — caught out of position
Late round 1v1 management — taking unwinnable duels
Pistol round meta — playing standard instead of map-specific cheese

positioning: Is the player crosshair placed at head level on the angle they're about to peek?
Is the player standing in a known common angle (pre-aimable) or off-angle (forces enemy to clear)?
Is the player exposed to multiple sightlines simultaneously? (1 angle = trade-able, 2+ = bad)
Is the player in a trade position with teammate (within 3 seconds of teammate's contact)?
If CT anchor: is the player playing off-default (not the obvious site spot)?
If T entry: is the player using cover to peek (slice the pie) or running straight in?
crosshair: Is the crosshair at head level?
Is the crosshair pre-aimed at the common angle (not the floor, not the sky)?
Is the player counter-strafing before shooting (releasing WASD before mouse 1)?
Is the spray pulled correctly for the weapon (AK pulls down + slight right; M4 pulls down)?
Is the player tapping at distance (>20m), bursting at mid-range, spraying at close)?
Is the player flicking onto target (high mouse sens) or tracking (low mouse sens)?
utility: Was a flash used before the player peeked? (or did they peek without prep)
Was a smoke used to block the angle the player crossed?
Was a molly used on the post-plant position?
Did the player throw utility BEFORE the execute or AFTER (correct = before)?
Was the utility coordinated with team or solo-thrown?
On retake: did the team use 2+ utility for the execute (correct) or 0 (suicide retake)?
decisions: Did the player buy aligned with team economy (full buy / eco / force)?
Did the player commit to the site execute or hesitate (commit halfway)?
Did the player rotate when called or stay on dead site?
Did the player save weapon on lost round (correct vs full buy CT)?
Did the player trade teammate's death (peek within 2s) or hesitate?
Did the player engage 1v2+ without team backup (force loss) or save?
Did the player commit to flanking lurk or fold back when team executes?

vocabulary: Spray pattern: bullet trajectory pattern for full-auto fire. Each weapon has unique pattern.
Counter-strafe: releasing WASD before mouse 1 for instant accuracy.
Pre-aim: aiming crosshair at the spot the enemy will appear before peeking.
Off-angle: a position the enemy isn't expecting you to hold (not the obvious spot).
Common angle: the standard position 95% of players hold — enemies pre-aim it.
Slice the pie: peeking by stepping out slowly, exposing 1 angle at a time.
Crossfire / Trade position: setup where 2 teammates cover same angle; one dies, the other trades.
Trade kill: killing the enemy who just killed your teammate within 2-3 seconds.
Eco round: round where team has $2000 or less; usually pistols + armor or full save.
Force buy: half-buy with limited rifles, used to break enemy economy after a loss.
Full buy: $4000+ team buy with rifles + full utility + armor.
Save round: round where team intentionally saves weapons for next round, often after losing pistol.
Anti-eco: full-buy round vs enemy eco; CT side stacks long, T side runs fast executes.
Pistol round: round 1 and round 13 (after halftime). Glock vs USP-S — winner often gets economy snowball.
Default: standard slow execute where T side takes map control over 60+ seconds.
Execute: planned fast hit on a site with full utility.
Lurk: solo player playing slow rotations, looking for flank trade kills.
Awper: player using the AWP sniper rifle.
Entry: first player into the site on execute.
Anchor: CT site holder who doesn't rotate.
IGL: In-Game Leader, calls mid-round adjustments.
Bomb plant: 3.2 second animation, 40 second timer.
Bait teammate: a teammate killed because you didn't trade them (and you survived).
Plant for: planting in a spot that helps a specific post-plant position.
Post-plant: 40-second timer after bomb plant — CT must retake, T holds.
Wallbang: shooting through walls / smokes / floors with high-penetration weapon (AWP, AK).
Stack: putting 3+ players on one site, leaving 1-2 on the other.
Default setup: balanced 2-1-2 (2 site, 1 mid, 2 site) CT setup.
Rush: T side runs fast at a site with no utility coordination — works on eco CTs.
Mid control: holding map mid (between A and B) for rotation info.
Boost: 2 players stand on each other for a higher angle / new sightline.
OneTap: single-shot headshot kill with a rifle.
Flick: fast crosshair adjustment for off-angle pick.
Tracking: keeping crosshair on enemy as they move.
Burst: 3-4 round burst with rifle (mid-range engagements).
Tap: single shot for long-range accuracy.
Spray: full-auto fire for close-range engagements.
Flashbang / Pop flash: grenade that blinds for 2-3 seconds.
Smoke grenade: blocks vision and bullets for ~18 seconds.
HE grenade: high-explosive, deals 50+ damage in a 6m radius.
Molotov / Incendiary / Molly: fire grenade, deals damage over time.
Decoy: distraction grenade that mimics gunfire.
Defuse kit: $400 utility that halves defuse time (10s → 5s).
Headshot: 4x damage multiplier; most rifles 1-shot to the head.
Tagging: hit detection slows enemy movement during fights.
